sections.heading: When to Use Sub-Feature Docs vs Feature Docs
sections.content:
Use the **feature_template/** docs (full PRD, design spec, user guide) for top-level features — major system capabilities like the viz dashboard, the indexer, or the MCP server.

Use the **sub_feature_template/** docs for components within a feature that are substantial enough to need their own documentation but don't warrant the full feature treatment. A sub-feature typically:

- Lives under a parent feature (e.g., doc-manager is a sub-feature of viz)
- Has its own user stories and backlog
- Already has or will have a design spec and user guide
- Doesn't need its own non-goals / scope shield (the parent feature's apply)

Examples of sub-features: doc manager, graph view, test dashboard, list view, detail panel.

sections.heading: Directory Structure
sections.content:
Sub-feature docs live under their parent feature:

```
docs/features/{feature}/sub_features/{sub_feature}/
    prd.json          ← Sub-feature PRD (this template)
    design.json       ← Design spec (use feature_template/design_spec_template)
    user-guide.json   ← User guide (use feature_template/user_guide_template)
```

The design spec and user guide templates from `feature_template/` work fine for sub-features — they don't need scaled-back versions. Only the PRD is lighter because sub-features inherit non-goals and scope from the parent.

sections.heading: Sub-Feature PRD Sections
sections.content:
The sub-feature PRD has 4 sections (vs 5 in the full PRD):

| Section | Purpose | Difference from full PRD |
|---|---|---|
| **Problem Statement** | Why this sub-feature exists | Same — keep it short (2-3 sentences) |
| **User Stories** | What users can do | Same format, prefixed US-XX-## (XX = sub-feature abbrev) |
| **Current Capabilities** | Status table of what's built | Replaces "V1 Requirements" — sub-features are usually partially built |
| **Backlog** | Planned enhancements | Replaces "Icebox" — less formal, one paragraph per item |

No **Non-Goals** section — sub-features inherit scope boundaries from the parent feature's PRD.

sections.heading: Available Templates
sections.content:
| Template | Path | Use for |
|---|---|---|
| Sub-feature PRD | `sub_feature_template/sub_feature_prd_template.json` | Scaled-back PRD for components within a feature |
| Full PRD | `feature_template/product_review_document_template.json` | Top-level feature PRDs |
| Design spec | `feature_template/design_spec_template.json` | Architecture and implementation details (any level) |
| User guide | `feature_template/user_guide_template.json` | End-user documentation (any level) |
| ADR | `architectural_desicion_record_template.json` | Architectural decisions |
